items.q: What is the significance of Duchamp's 'Fountain'?
items.correct: It challenged the definition of art - the artist's choice, not their skill, becomes the artwork.
items.random_wrong: It is a study of plumbing.
items.why: Students miss the conceptual point. Duchamp didn't make the urinal - he selected and presented it. The radical claim is that ART IS DESIGNATION, opening the door to all conceptual art.

items.correct: An object's design should be determined by its purpose.
items.misconception: Decoration is the most important aspect of design.
items.random_wrong: Designers must follow strict government forms.
items.why: Students invert the meaning. Sullivan's modernist principle prioritises FUNCTION over ornament - the chair's shape should serve sitting, not the other way around. Postmodernism later challenged this.

items.correct: Colours opposite each other on the colour wheel that create strong contrast.
items.misconception: Colours that look similar and harmonise gently.
items.random_wrong: Colours used to flatter the artist.
items.why: Students confuse 'complementary' with 'matching nicely'. In colour theory, complementary colours (red/green, blue/orange, yellow/purple) intensify each other through CONTRAST, not similarity.

items.q: Which photographer coined the phrase 'decisive moment'?
items.why: Correct answer: Henri Cartier-Bresson. “Robert Capa” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which setting most directly controls depth of field?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Aperture. “ISO” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Approximately what f-stop is considered a wide aperture providing shallow depth of field?
items.why: The real figure is 2 f-stop. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
